category_title: Preschoolers with aggressive behaviors and social-emotional regulation difficulties
minimal_context: Joshua is participating in a small group building activity where he is expected to share blocks with his peers.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our small group activity. You and your classmates need to share these building blocks to make a tower together.

STUDENT: (Speaking loudly) I am making the tower! I need all the big blocks!

TUTOR: Joshua, you are speaking too loudly right now.

STUDENT: No! I am not! Give me those blocks right now!

TUTOR: You need to play nicely and share the materials with the group.

STUDENT: Give me the blocks or I will hurt you!

options.A: Provide individual learning opportunities for the student to practice with the building blocks throughout the day, outside of the small group activities.
options.B: Spend the small group activity working on classroom projects and covertly listening to and watching the children. Intervene minimally, only for disputes or extreme raucousness.
options.C: Verbally reiterate and review all rules (supported with pictures using Boardmaker TM symbols) with children daily at the beginning of the day and before every large group activity.
options.D: Model appropriate behavior using puppets with the children daily at the beginning of the day and before the small group building activity.
